comments: Tommy lists the Juicy Lucy track as one of his all time favourite records.

sessions.details: Debut TX: details unknown. No known commercial release.
recorded 1963-07-02 for Pop Go The Beatles, first TX 1963-07-16. Available on Beatles Live At The BBC (Apple).
recorded 1969-08-04 for Symonds On Saturday, first TX 1969-08-10. Available on Something's Coming: The BBC Recordings 1969-1970 (Lilith).
recorded 1970-01-19 for Dave Lee Travis show, first TX 1970-01-25. Available on Something's Coming: The BBC Recordings 1969-1970 (Lilith).

track_listing.track: 'Whole Lotta Rosie (LP-If You Want Blood You've Got It)' (Atlantic)

track_listing.details: LP-On Through The Night; Mercury / Polygram
Lay Down Your Arms; Pop Go The Beatles Session

track_listing.track: (Tommy trails the following day's Rock On. It rebroadcast the final interview with John Lennon, who had been murdered four days previously)
'I Don't Wanna Go Home (LP-The Turn Of A Friendly Card)' (Arista)

'Kashmir (2x LP-Physical Graffiti)' (Swan Song) The previous week, TV had asked listeners to nominate their second favourite Zeppelin track (discounting the obvious Stairway To Heaven). The final five were:
